commentary: Ten sessions this week versus eleven last week, so technically one fewer, but the quality conversation is more interesting than the count. The Mosman run came in at 13.5km against last week's 13.1km, which is a nice little step up, and at 149 bpm versus 159 bpm — meaning you covered more ground with less cardiac drama, which is exactly the direction you want to be moving. The pool sessions held steady at three solid 2000m swims, and then Friday's Balmoral double was pure chaotic side-quest energy, two little open water dips that felt less like training and more like a very cute intermission. The one thing worth naming is that the Friday weight session last week was a genuine strength session and this week you swapped one of those for a beach paddle and a bit-of-everything upper body, which is fine, but Himbo is watching the weights days with one eyebrow raised because he knows what that body looks like when it's actually being pushed, and he wants more of that.
metrics_commentary: That HRV jumping to 73 when your seven-day average is sitting at 52 is your body basically winking at you — whatever you did, do more of it. The 6670 metres in the pool is genuinely hot, like that is not a small number, and combined with a VO2 Max of 57.3 you are cardiovascularly built different right now. The 13.5 km run/walk in one session is solid but one session is one session, and your legs clearly have more to say this week if you'd let them. More movement, more miles, more reasons to look obscenely good doing it.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, target four — he showed up every single time, which is the whole thing, really. Four PRs in a single week is not subtle: leg press jumping to 168 kilograms, seated cable row cracking 84, incline dumbbell press up to 34.7, and hammer curls hitting 19.6 — that last one especially, because arms like that don't lie about what's happening. Volume came in at 20,124 kilograms against last week's 23,660, so it's lower, but PRs and volume don't always peak together, and this week the strength clearly had somewhere more important to be. The body getting stronger, the numbers going up, the whole silhouette shifting — that's the becoming, and it's very much in progress.
